Survey/review paper ('State of the Art on Neural Rendering'). It is a comprehensive survey covering neural rendering broadly including faces, bodies, scenes, relighting, etc. It does not propose a specific method for digital human creation.

General survey/STAR report on advances in neural rendering covering NeRF, scene representations, and rendering broadly. Not specifically focused on digital human reconstruction, generation, or animation.

StyleGAN is a general 2D GAN architecture for image generation. While it generates face images and introduces the FFHQ dataset, it does not create 3D digital humans, avatars, or digital human assets. It is a 2D image synthesis method without 3D representation, animation, or avatar creation capabilities.

Survey/review paper on 3D face reconstruction from uncalibrated images. Does not propose a specific method for creating digital humans; instead reviews and categorizes existing approaches (statistical model fitting, photometry, deep learning). Cannot meaningfully fill pipeline-specific fields.

GS-LRM is a general-purpose large reconstruction model for 3D Gaussian Splatting from sparse posed images. It is trained on Objaverse (synthetic objects) and RealEstate10K (indoor scenes) and does not target digital humans. No human-specific components, datasets, or evaluations are included.

This is a 2D image dataset paper (500K Korean hairstyle images) for 2D hair editing tasks (virtual hair dyeing, hairstyle transfer, hairstyle classification). It does not create 3D digital human avatars or 3D hair assets. The applications are 2D image manipulation, not 3D reconstruction or generation.

Hairstyle classification method, not digital human creation. Predicts hairstyle category labels from single images using a classification network trained on synthetic data. Does not reconstruct, generate, or animate any 3D human geometry, appearance, or assets.

VoxCeleb is a large-scale speaker identification/verification dataset (1,251 celebrities, 153,516 utterances from YouTube). It focuses on audio-based speaker recognition using CNN architectures on spectrograms. It does not create digital human avatars or 3D assets. While it is commonly used as a training data source by talking-head synthesis methods, the paper itself proposes no avatar generation or 3D reconstruction.

This paper proposes a stochastic ray-tracing method for rendering 3D clouds of transparent Gaussian primitives. It is a general-purpose rendering technique for 3D Gaussian splatting (demonstrated on seashells, rooms, furniture, etc.) and does not create, reconstruct, or animate digital humans or human-related assets.

RaySplats is a ray-tracing based rendering method for 3D Gaussian Splatting that enables lighting effects (shadows, reflections, transparency). It is a general-purpose rendering technique evaluated on generic scene datasets (Mip-NeRF360, Tanks and Temples, Deep Blending) and does not specifically create, reconstruct, or animate digital humans or human-related assets.

InteractVLM estimates 3D contact points between human bodies and objects from single in-the-wild images using Vision-Language Models. It is a perception/contact estimation method for human-object interaction understanding, not a method for creating, animating, or rendering digital human avatars or 3D assets.

Sapiens is a family of foundation models for human-centric vision tasks (2D pose estimation, body-part segmentation, depth estimation, surface normal prediction). It is a perception/estimation model pretrained on 300M+ human images, not a method for creating 3D digital human avatars or 3D assets. It does not generate, reconstruct, or render 3D human representations.

General survey on controllable video generation covering text-to-video, camera motion, depth maps, and pose control broadly. Not specifically focused on digital human reconstruction, generation, or avatar creation.

General STAR report on diffusion models for visual computing broadly, covering images, videos, 3D objects, locomotion, and 4D scenes. Not specifically focused on digital human reconstruction or avatar creation.
General survey on 3D human avatar modeling covering reconstruction and generation broadly. Survey/review papers do not introduce a specific method for creating digital humans.
Deepfake detection method. DiffusionFake enhances generalization in deepfake detection via guided stable diffusion, not creating/animating digital humans.
Deepfake detection benchmark. DigiFakeAV is a dataset and detection baseline (DigiShield) for multimodal deepfake detection, not a method for creating digital humans.
Face manipulation detection. Detects photoshopped face warping manipulations, not creating/animating digital humans.
Deepfake video detection method. ID-Reveal learns temporal facial features for detecting deepfakes, not creating digital humans.
AI-generated image detection. ZED is a zero-shot entropy-based detector for AI-generated images, not a method for creating digital humans.
Image forgery detection. ForensicTransfer detects image manipulations via domain adaptation, not creating digital humans.
Multilingual speech translation system. Seamless is about expressive and streaming speech-to-speech/text translation, not about digital human creation or rendering.

Wan is a general-purpose large-scale video generative foundation model (diffusion transformer) for broad video generation tasks. It is not specifically about creating, animating, or rendering digital human avatars or human-specific assets.

PhysAnimator generates physically plausible cartoon/anime-style animations from static anime illustrations using physics simulation and sketch-guided video diffusion. It targets general cartoon animation (deformable body simulation on illustrations), not digital human avatar creation or human-specific asset generation.

General text-to-image generation method. HanDiffuser improves hand quality in text-to-image diffusion models by injecting SMPL-Body and MANO-Hand parameters, but it generates 2D images of humans (not avatars, not 3D reconstructions, not animatable digital humans). It is a general image synthesis method with improved hand realism, not a digital human creation/animation/rendering method.

General text/pose-to-image generation method. This paper improves hand region quality in diffusion-based human image synthesis using multi-modal control (skeleton, depth, surface normal) and a Region-Aware Cycle Loss. It generates 2D images of humans conditioned on poses, not 3D digital human avatars or assets.

Human pose estimation and generation method focused on self-contact. Goliath-SC provides a dataset and generative model for self-contact human pose priors conditioned on body shape, used to improve single-view pose estimation. It does not create, render, or animate digital human avatars or assets.

Fast-Vid2Vid is a general video-to-video synthesis compression/acceleration framework using spatial-temporal knowledge distillation. While it demonstrates on Sketch2Face and Pose2Body tasks, it is fundamentally a GAN compression method and does not propose a new method for creating or animating digital human avatars.

Video LDM (Align Your Latents) is a general-purpose latent video diffusion model for high-resolution video generation including driving scenes and text-to-video. It is not specific to digital human avatar creation, reconstruction, or animation.

Stable Video Diffusion is a general-purpose latent video diffusion model for text-to-video and image-to-video generation. It presents a data curation and three-stage training framework but is not specific to digital human avatar creation or animation.

ControlNet is a general-purpose neural network architecture for adding spatial conditioning controls (edges, depth, segmentation, human pose, etc.) to pretrained text-to-image diffusion models like Stable Diffusion. While it demonstrates human pose as one condition among many, it is not a method for creating, reconstructing, or animating digital human avatars.

BodyNet is a 3D human body shape estimation method that predicts volumetric body shape from a single image and fits SMPL to the output. It is a pose and shape estimation/reconstruction pipeline, not a method for creating animatable or renderable digital human avatars.

SPIN is a 3D human pose and shape estimation method that combines regression-based and optimization-based SMPL fitting in a self-improving training loop. It estimates SMPL parameters from single images for pose/shape benchmarks, not for creating digital human avatars.

PARE is a 3D human pose and shape estimation method using part-guided soft attention for robust SMPL parameter regression under occlusion. It is a body estimation method, not an avatar creation or rendering method.

EgoHMR is a probabilistic human mesh recovery method from egocentric views using scene-conditioned diffusion for SMPL pose estimation. It models pose distributions for truncated bodies but is a pose/shape estimation method, not an avatar creation method.

Pure 3D human pose and shape estimation method. ProHMR uses normalizing flows to predict a distribution of plausible 3D human poses from 2D images/keypoints. It does not create, render, or animate digital human avatars -- it is a pose estimation method that outputs SMPL mesh parameters for downstream tasks like multi-view reconstruction and model fitting.

Multi-person 3D pose and shape estimation method. BEV regresses 3D human meshes with depth reasoning from a single image using a bird's-eye-view representation. It estimates SMPL body parameters for detection and depth ordering, but does not create, render, or animate digital human avatars.

General 4D video generation framework for arbitrary scenes (not human-specific). 4Real-Video generates grids of video frames varying in time and viewpoint using a two-stream diffusion architecture. While it can be applied to scenes containing humans, the method is designed for general objects and scenes (e.g., trucks, lego bricks, real-world captures) and does not specifically address digital human avatar creation, animation, or rendering.

SURREAL is a synthetic dataset for training perception models (human pose estimation, depth estimation, body part segmentation). It does not create, animate, or render digital human avatars -- it generates training data for downstream computer vision tasks.

AIST++ is a dataset and method for music-conditioned 3D dance motion generation. It generates skeletal motion sequences, not digital human avatars or their appearance. The focus is on motion synthesis rather than avatar creation/rendering.

GRAB is a dataset of whole-body human grasping of objects. It captures MoCap data of hands/bodies interacting with objects and trains a generative network for hand grasps. It is focused on hand-object interaction rather than creating or rendering digital human avatars.

DAViD trains vision models on synthetic data for dense prediction tasks (depth estimation, surface normal estimation, foreground segmentation). It is not about creating, animating, or rendering digital human avatars -- it uses synthetic humans as training data for general perception tasks.

VFHQ is a dataset and benchmark for video face super-resolution (upscaling low-res face videos). It does not create, animate, or render digital human avatars or assets -- it is a general image/video processing method applied to faces.

General-purpose physics solver (block coordinate descent for implicit Euler integration) for elastic body dynamics, particle-based simulations, and rigid bodies. Not specifically about creating, animating, or rendering digital human avatars or garments/hair assets.

Body shape estimation/reconstruction method from clothed 3D scan sequences. Estimates underlying body shape under clothing using SMPL body model, not creating/animating/rendering digital human avatars or garment assets.

AnyDressing is a 2D multi-garment virtual dressing/try-on method using latent diffusion models to generate 2D images of people wearing different garment combinations. It does not create, animate, or render 3D digital human avatars or 3D garment/hair assets.

WildVidFit is a 2D video virtual try-on method that generates videos of people wearing garments using image-based controlled diffusion models. It does not create 3D digital human avatars or 3D garment/hair assets.
Fashion-VDM is a 2D video diffusion model for virtual try-on that generates try-on videos from person video and garment image. It does not create 3D digital human avatars or 3D garment/hair assets.
DPIDM is a 2D video virtual try-on method using diffusion models with dynamic pose interaction for temporal consistency. It generates 2D try-on videos, not 3D digital human avatars or 3D assets.
General few-shot 3D reconstruction method for arbitrary object categories (chairs, cars, airplanes, etc.) using ShapeNet. Not specific to digital humans.

Dataset and benchmark paper for 3D interacting hand pose estimation (InterHand2.6M with 2.6M labeled frames). Primarily a dataset/pose estimation contribution, not a method for creating/animating/rendering digital human avatars.
General-purpose GAN architecture improvement (StyleGAN3/Alias-Free GAN) addressing aliasing in generative networks. Not specifically about digital human avatar creation, animation, or rendering.
Survey paper on neural fields in visual computing. This is a comprehensive review/survey, not a method for creating digital humans.
Deepfake detection and media authentication method using semi-fragile watermarking. Not about creating, animating, or rendering digital humans.
Deepfake detection benchmark and dataset (FaceForensics++). Focused on detecting manipulated facial images, not on creating or rendering digital humans.

This paper presents SHAPY, a neural network for 3D body shape regression (SMPL-X parameters) from a single RGB image using linguistic shape attributes and anthropometric measurements. It focuses on body shape estimation, not on creating, animating, or rendering digital human avatars. It does not generate images or 3D renderable representations of humans.
Foundational hair scattering/shading model paper; does not create avatars or hair assets, but provides a BSDF model for hair rendering.
General-purpose image generation/editing model (FLUX.1), not specifically about digital humans or avatars.
Dataset paper (Ryerson Audio-Visual Database of Emotional Speech and Song); provides a benchmark dataset, not an avatar or asset creation method.
Cloth material measurement and elastic model fitting paper; provides data-driven cloth simulation parameters, not digital human avatars or assets.
Cloth simulation model parameter estimation paper; provides methods for fitting simulation parameters to real cloth, not digital human avatars or assets.
Fabric material modeling and parameter estimation paper; provides constitutive models and fitting methods for woven fabrics, not digital human avatars or assets.
Dataset and benchmark paper (Codec Avatar Studio); provides paired multi-modal capture datasets of 256 subjects, not an avatar/asset creation method itself.
Virtual try-on video generation method, not creating animatable avatars or reusable assets
Dense articulated real-time tracking system, not creating avatars or assets
AGI levels taxonomy/classification paper. Not related to digital human creation.
General deformable NeRF for arbitrary dynamic scenes. Not human-specific.
General deformable NeRF for casually captured scenes. Not human-specific.
Text-to-motion retrieval method. Does not create human representations.
Dataset paper (4500 subjects, MVHumanNet). No novel avatar creation method.
Dataset paper (OpenHumanVid). Primary contribution is the dataset.
Dataset and benchmark paper (DNA-Rendering). No novel avatar creation method.
Motion generation paper, no visual human avatar creation
Motion generation paper, no visual human avatar creation
Motion generation paper, no visual human avatar creation
Physical garment fabrication pipeline for reusing sewing patterns, not digital asset creation
